# Villa Lithos Porto Rafti

> Villa Lithos Porto Rafti is a 9-bedroom, 800 m² luxury villa in Porto Rafti, Attica, Greece, located 16 km (a 20-minute drive) from Athens International Airport. The property sleeps 22 guests across nine bedrooms and 8.5 bathrooms on a 5,000 m² private estate, with a heated infinity pool, outdoor sauna, jacuzzi, padel court, private gym, and elevator. It is managed by Goldenberg Luxe and is bookable on Booking.com, Airbnb, and direct.

## Key facts

- Name: Villa Lithos Porto Rafti
- Location: Vravronos 70, Porto Rafti 19003, Attica, Greece
- Coordinates: 37.9022, 24.0224
- Bedrooms: 9
- Bathrooms: 8.5
- Maximum capacity: 22 guests
- Indoor area: 800 m²
- Plot size: 5,000 m²
- Floors: 4
- Distance to Athens International Airport: 16 km, approximately 20 minutes by car
- Distance to central Athens (Acropolis): 37 km via Attiki Odos, approximately 40 minutes
- Distance to nearest beach (Avlaki / Erotospilia): 1.5 km, approximately 3 minutes by car
- Distance to Rafina Port (Cyclades ferries): approximately 20 minutes by car
- Check-in: 15:00
- Check-out: 11:00
- Pets allowed: No
- Smoking allowed: No
- Managed by: Goldenberg Luxe
- Languages spoken: English, Greek, Hebrew

## Amenities

- Heated infinity pool with panoramic sea views
- Jacuzzi
- Outdoor sauna
- Private padel court
- Private gym
- Private elevator across four floors
- Designer kitchen with walk-in pantry
- Two living rooms (ground floor and lower level)
- Sea view and mountain view
- WiFi and air conditioning throughout
- BBQ area
- Workspace for remote work or corporate retreats
- Secure private parking inside the gated estate

## About Porto Rafti

Porto Rafti is a coastal town on the eastern shore of Attica, Greece, on the Aegean Sea. The bay is naturally sheltered, which produces calm shallow water near the shore, well suited to families with young children. The area is one of the closest mainland coastal destinations to Athens, with the international airport approximately 16 km away. The wider region is one of the most historically rich parts of Europe, with the Temple of Poseidon at Cape Sounion, the sanctuary of Brauron, the battlefield of Marathon, and the Acropolis of Athens all within a short drive.
